steps.title: Set up the product
steps.content: The function computes the product of an input number and a number that is 62 more than that input. If the input is $x$, then: $$f(x) = x(x + 62)$$

steps.title: Expand to find the function equation
steps.content: Apply the distributive property: $$f(x) = x(x + 62) = x^2 + 62x$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$f(x) = x^2 + x + 62$': The $+x$ term has no origin in the problem — this doesn't come from $x(x+62)$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(x) = x^2 + 62$': Missing the $62x$ cross-term; this would be $x^2 + 62$, not a product of $x$ and $x+62$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(x) = x^2 + 62x$': Exactly $x(x+62)$ expanded — the product of the number and the number 62 more than it. ✓
  • Choice D — '$f(x) = x^2 + 62x + 62$': The extra $+62$ constant doesn't come from the multiplication. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
